title: RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 record + lease acquisition primitive
goal: Implement the durable RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 record (Postgres) and the lease acquisition + extension + release primitive that keeps exactly one active executor on any exclusive or handoff-style mailbox thread. Closes the folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Executor-Routing-Claim-Lease-v1 stub's 'one lawful ownership layer' requirement.
expected_diff_shape: New Postgres migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/NNN_role_mailbox_claim_lease.sql; new Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/lease.rs with `LeaseManager::acquire / extend / release / takeover` methods + `LeaseError` enum; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_lease_tests.rs.
owned_files_note: MT-180's role_mailbox_claim_lease schema was folded into the 0022_role_mailbox_threads_messages.sql migration (see SQL line 51 'MT-180 RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 schema...') rather than landing as a separate NNN_role_mailbox_claim_lease.sql migration. MT-180 therefore co-owns the 0022 migration with MT-176. The Rust LeaseManager primitive remains owned solely by MT-180 at src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/lease.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-177 repo + Postgres infrastructure available so the lease table can FK to role_mailbox_thread.
implementation_notes: Per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.176] lines 6151-6156: define `RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 { lease_id: Uuid v7, thread_id: Uuid v7 (FK), holder_executor_kind: ExecutorKind, holder_role_id: RoleId, holder_session_id: Uuid v7, acquired_at_utc, expires_at_utc, released_at_utc: Option, takeover_of: Option<Uuid v7> (FK to predecessor lease), takeover_reason: Option<String> }`. Postgres migration creates the table with a partial unique index `WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL AND expires_at_utc > now()` keyed by thread_id — this enforces 'one active unexpired lease per thread' at the database level per spec line 6156. `LeaseManager::acquire(thread_id, requesting: LeaseRequest) -> Result<RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1, LeaseError>` runs inside a transaction: (1) SELECT thread FOR UPDATE; (2) check thread.claim_mode allows requesting.executor_kind via executor_kind_allowlist; (3) check no active lease (or, if claim_mode=Handoff, allow takeover per takeover_policy); (4) INSERT lease row. LeaseError variants: `LeaseHeldByOther { current_holder }`, `ExecutorKindNotAllowed`, `ThreadInTerminalState`, `Conflict`. `extend(lease_id, extra_secs)` is idempotent and only succeeds if lease is still unexpired and unreleased. `release(lease_id)` sets released_at_utc and is a no-op if already released. `takeover(thread_id, requesting, predecessor_lease_id, reason)` permitted only when thread.takeover_policy allows (TakeoverPolicy enum: Never|OnLeaseExpiry|AlwaysWithReason|OperatorOnly); writes new lease row with takeover_of and takeover_reason set, and force-releases the predecessor with reason recorded. Tests (use Postgres test container): (a) acquire on fresh thread succeeds; (b) second acquire returns LeaseHeldByOther; (c) acquire after lease expiry succeeds; (d) takeover with Never policy returns error; (e) parallel acquire from 8 tasks — exactly one wins; (f) extend after expiry returns error; (g) audit row chain is queryable via lease_id ancestry.
risk_if_missed: Without a lease primitive, the cluster X preservation slice's central invariant (one active executor per exclusive thread, no races between local + cloud models + reviewers + validators + operators + automation) is silently violated. The folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Executor-Routing-Claim-Lease-v1 intent is lost.
validator_focus: Partial unique index prevents two active leases on the same thread at the database level (not application level); parallel-acquire test asserts exactly-one-winner; takeover policy enforced atomically with predecessor release.

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Normalized handoff expires_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision before bundle hashing.

note: MT-177 targeted repository lifecycle round trip passed.

Focused Postgres/live-kernel commands were run, not a full workspace cargo test.

minimum_controls: Database-level partial unique index proven by an INSERT-conflict test bypassing the LeaseManager — must fail with a Postgres unique-violation error.
Takeover audit chain queryable via recursive CTE so an auditor can reconstruct the full ownership history of any thread.
Lease extension cannot bypass expiry (extend after expires_at_utc returns LeaseExpired).

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: MT-180 RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 record + lease acquisition primitive transitioned PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. The record type RoleMailboxClaimLeaseV1 (Rust struct + serde, full field set including takeover_of/takeover_reason) and the in-process LeaseManager (acquire/extend/release/takeover) already existed at src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/lease.rs from prior cluster X.1 work. The MT-180 implementation added the Postgres-backed primitive on src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs: acquire_lease (FOR UPDATE on thread + sweep expired-but-unreleased leases + 23505 unique_violation handling), extend_lease (FOR UPDATE on lease + expiry guard + AlreadyReleased guard), release_lease (idempotent), takeover_lease (atomic predecessor force-release + new lease INSERT with takeover_of/takeover_reason provenance + policy gates Never/OnLeaseExpiry/AlwaysWithReason/OperatorOnly), get_active_lease_for_thread (router surface), and list_lease_chain_for_thread (recursive CTE that satisfies red_team.minimum_controls #2 takeover audit chain queryable). A dedicated MT-180 adversarial test file was added at tests/role_mailbox_lease_tests.rs (the owned-files path) covering pure-Rust adversarial: serde round-trip full field set, serde round-trip with None provenance fields preserving the explicit-null wire shape, wrong-format executor_kind reject, oversize takeover_reason shape stability, missing-required-field reject, Uuid v7 mint-site enforcement (HBR-INT-008), LeaseError variant Display distinctness, in-process extend-after-expiry reject, CX-503R compile-time PgPool-only constructor guard, TakeoverPolicy snake_case wire-shape snapshot, active-predicate composition snapshot. Postgres-gated adversarial: acquire on fresh thread succeeds, second acquire returns LeaseHeldByOther, acquire after expiry succeeds via sweep, acquire rejects disallowed executor kind, acquire rejects terminal thread, acquire on missing thread NotFound, eight-parallel-caller race exactly-one-winner, direct-INSERT-bypass blocked by partial unique index with 23505 unique_violation (red_team #1), extend after expiry rejects (red_team #3), extend extends expiry, release frees thread + reacquire + idempotent re-release, release on unknown lease NotFound, takeover Never policy rejects, takeover atomically releases predecessor + new active lease verifies (validator_focus), takeover chain queryable via recursive CTE 3-deep (red_team #2), takeover OperatorOnly rejects non-operator, takeover OnLeaseExpiry rejects unexpired predecessor, concurrent acquire+release safe, get_active_lease returns None when released, repo-only acquire/extend/takeover/release/list round-trip.

out_of_scope_edits.change: Added [[test]] block declaring role_mailbox_lease_tests with required-features=['test-utils'] mirroring the role_mailbox_repo_tests pattern. Necessary because the test binary cannot enforce required-features without a declared [[test]] entry; Cargo auto-discovery falls back to defaults which omits explicit test-utils gating. Same waiver class as MT-177r2.

contract_deviation_notes: PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT: MT-180 expected_diff_shape and owned_files name `src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/lease.rs` and `migrations/NNN_role_mailbox_claim_lease.sql`. The existing folded implementation lives under `role_mailbox_v1/lease.rs` and the lease table was already in migration `0022_role_mailbox_threads_messages.sql` (rows 53-71) from prior cluster X.1 work (same PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT class flagged in MT-176/MT-177). No new product code under role_mailbox/ was created — that would duplicate the authoritative implementation. No new migration was created — the lease table already exists with the spec-required partial unique index `idx_role_mailbox_claim_lease_active` keyed on `thread_id WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL`. Operator/Integration Validator decision needed on path-shape drift vs. the actual role_mailbox_v1/ tree + the migration's location.

PARTIAL_UNIQUE_INDEX_PREDICATE_DIVERGENCE: The contract implementation_notes specify the partial unique index `WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL AND expires_at_utc > now()`. Postgres partial unique indexes cannot use mutable functions like NOW() — only IMMUTABLE expressions are allowed. The existing migration 0022 uses `WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL` only. The application layer compensates by sweeping expired-but-unreleased leases inside acquire_lease before the INSERT (mark them released so the index admits the new row). This is functionally equivalent to the spec intent (exactly-one-active-lease-per-thread) but achieves it through application-layer sweep + DB-level partial index, rather than the unimplementable NOW()-based index. The sweep is covered by the postgres_acquire_after_expiry_succeeds test.

proof_commands.note: 11/11 default tests pass; 20 Postgres-gated tests #[ignore]-ed pending POSTGRES_TEST_URL.

proof_commands.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_lease_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests --test role_mailbox_v1_tests -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
proof_commands.note: Adjacent role_mailbox_repo_tests (7 passed, 11 ignored) and role_mailbox_v1_tests (9 passed, 2 ignored) still pass alongside the new MT-180 test binary; no regression from the repo.rs lease-method additions or the Cargo.toml [[test]] addition.

residual_risks: 20 Postgres-gated adversarial tests (8-parallel-caller race, direct-INSERT-bypass 23505 proof, after-expiry sweep, takeover chain recursive CTE, OperatorOnly/OnLeaseExpiry policy gates, concurrent acquire+release race, etc.) require POSTGRES_TEST_URL to actually validate in-database FOR UPDATE semantics + the partial unique index. Without the live-Postgres run the contract's red_team.minimum_controls #1 and #2 are structurally satisfied but not executionally verified — same Postgres-gating class as MT-190 / MT-197 / MT-177.
PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT: owned_files name role_mailbox/lease.rs but the implementation is under role_mailbox_v1/lease.rs (folded preservation slice). Integration Validator decision needed.

PARTIAL_UNIQUE_INDEX_PREDICATE_DIVERGENCE: contract's NOW()-based partial index predicate cannot be expressed in Postgres; the application-layer sweep + simpler partial index achieves the same invariant. Integration Validator decision needed on whether the spec text should be tightened to match Postgres reality.
Cargo.toml [[test]] addition is out-of-owned-files; operator waiver requested. Alternative (Cargo auto-discovery) would silently default to feature set [] and bypass the test-utils requirement.

ANTI-COORDINATION: 2 other agents running in parallel were declared in the dispatch envelope. The cargo check + cargo test runs reported only pre-existing 55 warnings; no unrelated-module compile errors observed during this session.

extend_lease applies its time check using server-side `Utc::now()` rather than reading the DB's NOW(). For the typical case clock skew is bounded; for hostile-clock test scenarios a Postgres-side `WHERE expires_at_utc > NOW()` could be added to the UPDATE — out of MT-180 scope, flagged for MT-181/MT-184.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Lease primitive (acquire/extend/release/takeover) lands with FOR UPDATE serialisation + 23505 unique_violation handling + recursive-CTE takeover chain + idempotent extend/release. Partial unique index `idx_role_mailbox_claim_lease_active WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL` enforces 'one active lease per thread' at DB layer. Pure-Rust adversarial tests 11/11 green; 20 Postgres-gated tests proven against live Postgres in the 2026-05-25 continuation. Acceptable predicate divergence vs. contract NOW()-based index (Postgres immutability constraint).
